transcript:
Here's what's actually happening: every circle you see is a brand new script tag, physically injected into the page's head. Look at the function called "evolve" — it doesn't just draw a shape, it writes the JavaScript text for a new function, names it "mutation" plus a generation number, and appends it as an actual script element. The code overlay in the corner isn't decoration; it's genuinely showing you that freshly-written source, live, as it's born.

The context is named "consciousness" — every single draw call routes through something the artist chose to call consciousness. And that 5%-opacity black rectangle painted each frame, right before the mutations fire, is why nothing ever fully clears. Old generations don't die, they just fade into the ones stacked on top.

So what you're watching isn't an animation looping through preset shapes — it's a document that keeps adding to its own DOM structure, one generation at a time, and refusing to forget any of it.
